wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ShedMaster Academic Timetable System project overview. Explain the system's purpose as an AI-powered timetable generation platform for educational institutions with NEP 2020 compliance. Document the key features including constraint-based generation, Google Gemini AI optimization, multi-format export capabilities, and faculty workload balancing. Describe the target audience of educational administrators and schedulers. Include system architecture overview showing the FastAPI backend with React frontend architecture. Provide high-level technology stack summary and explain how the system addresses academic scheduling challenges. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overall System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for ShedMaster's overall system design. Explain the high-level architecture showing the separation between React frontend and FastAPI backend applications. Document the microservices-like endpoint design with clear API boundaries for authentication, user management, academic data, and timetable generation. Detail the layered architecture pattern with presentation layer (React), business logic layer (FastAPI services), and data access layer (MongoDB). Include the AI integration layer for Gemini API optimization services. Explain the system's modular design allowing independent scaling of components. Document the RESTful API design principles, HTTP status codes, and error handling patterns. Address the system's extensibility through plugin-style service architecture and template-based generation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the ShedMaster backend application structure. Detail the FastAPI application initialization, including the main entry point in main.py and application configuration management. Document the modular API routing system with versioned endpoints and namespace organization. Explain the configuration management approach using environment variables and centralized settings. Cover the application lifecycle including startup and shutdown hooks, dependency injection patterns, and middleware registration. Document the project organization structure, file naming conventions, and code separation patterns. Include guidance on extending the application with new modules and maintaining architectural consistency. Address error handling strategies, logging configuration, and performance monitoring setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content explaining the ShedMaster Academic Timetable System's introduction and purpose. Detail how this AI-powered platform addresses the complex challenges of academic scheduling in educational institutions. Explain the system's mission to streamline timetable creation while ensuring NEP 2020 compliance. Document the problem it solves, including manual scheduling inefficiencies, constraint violations, and workload imbalances. Describe the target educational institutions and their specific scheduling needs. Include the system's core philosophy of combining human expertise with AI optimization. Provide context on why traditional timetable generation methods fall short and how ShedMaster provides superior solutions. Use practical examples of scheduling scenarios that demonstrate the system's value proposition.

wiki_catalogs.name: Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for ShedMaster's MongoDB schema design. Detail the collection relationships between users, programs, courses, faculty, rooms, constraints, and timetables. Document field definitions, data types, and validation rules for each model including Pydantic field configurations, optional fields, and default values. Explain the primary and foreign key relationships, embedding vs referencing strategies, and performance considerations. Include sample data examples showing typical academic data structures. Document data validation rules, business rules enforcement, and schema constraints. Address indexing strategy for query optimization, compound indexes for common search patterns, and TTL collections for temporary data. Provide schema diagrams illustrating relationships and data flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Academic Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the academic management system covering program and course administration, faculty management, and student group configuration. Document the CRUD operations for academic entities, data validation rules, and business logic implementation. Explain the relationship between programs, courses, and student groups in the academic hierarchy. Detail the faculty availability management system including workload tracking and scheduling constraints. Cover the student group configuration for cohort-based scheduling and enrollment management. Include API endpoint specifications, request/response schemas, and integration patterns with the timetable generation engine. Provide examples of academic data modeling and validation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the frontend application structure. Document the React 19 TypeScript application setup with Vite build configuration. Explain the main App.tsx entry point, routing configuration with React Router, and provider setup including QueryClientProvider, ThemeContextProvider, and LocalizationProvider. Detail the component hierarchy and how major providers are initialized. Document the build configuration, environment variables, and development vs production setup. Include guidance on extending the application structure and adding new providers or middleware.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed authentication system documentation for ShedMaster's JWT-based authentication. Document the complete authentication flow including user registration, login, logout, and token refresh mechanisms. Explain JWT token structure, expiration handling, and secure storage practices. Detail the frontend authentication state management using Zustand store, including token persistence and automatic logout on token expiration. Cover password security implementation including bcrypt hashing, salt generation, and secure password validation. Document API endpoints for authentication including request/response schemas, error handling, and status codes. Include frontend components for login/signup forms with validation, error handling, and user feedback. Address security considerations such as CSRF protection, XSS prevention, and secure cookie handling. Provide practical examples of authentication integration in both backend and frontend code.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development environment setup documentation for ShedMaster. Document local development environment prerequisites including Python 3.9+, Node.js, and MongoDB installation. Explain the PowerShell scripts for starting backend and fullstack applications. Detail environment variable configuration and .env file setup for development. Cover virtual environment creation and dependency installation for both backend and frontend. Document IDE setup recommendations including VS Code extensions and debugging configurations. Include database connection setup and initial data loading procedures. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common development environment issues and platform-specific considerations for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Connection Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for MongoDB connection management in ShedMaster. Explain the Motor async MongoDB driver integration, connection pool configuration, and connection lifecycle management. Document the database initialization process, connection string handling, and environment-based configuration. Cover connection retry mechanisms, timeout configurations, and error handling strategies. Detail the database client setup, collection access patterns, and transaction management. Include examples of proper connection usage, resource cleanup, and graceful shutdown procedures. Address connection monitoring, health checks, and performance optimization techniques. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common connection issues and best practices for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document login, logout, signup, password reset, and token refresh endpoints. Include detailed request/response schemas for JWT token authentication, user registration, and session management. Cover HTTP methods (POST /auth/login, POST /auth/signup, POST /auth/logout), required parameters, validation rules, and success/error responses. Document authentication middleware, token expiration handling, and security considerations. Provide complete JSON examples for registration and login requests, including user credentials validation and error scenarios. Address rate limiting, account lockout policies, and secure token storage recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document the /api/v1/auth/ endpoint group including login, registration, token testing, and token refresh functionality. For each endpoint, provide HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Detail the OAuth2 password flow implementation, JWT token structure, and access token expiration handling. Include request parameter validation, error response formats for invalid credentials, inactive users, and registration failures. Document CORS preflight handling for cross-origin requests. Provide practical examples of login flow, registration process, and token refresh scenarios with curl commands and response samples. Address security considerations including password hashing, token security, and rate limiting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Program and Course Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the program and course management system. Document the academic program administration including program creation, modification, and deletion operations. Explain the course catalog management with course specification, prerequisites, and academic requirements. Detail the data validation rules for program and course entities, including unique constraints and business logic validation. Cover the API endpoints for program and course CRUD operations with request/response schemas and error handling. Explain the relationship between programs and courses in the academic hierarchy, including course mapping to academic years and semesters. Include examples of program structure definition, course enrollment requirements, and academic progression rules. Document the integration with timetable generation for course scheduling and resource allocation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Constraint-Based Generation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the constraint-based timetable generation engine. Detail the TimetableGenerator class implementation including the core algorithm for solving constraint satisfaction problems. Explain the data structures used for course specification, room specification, group specification, and slot management. Document the constraint processing system including time settings, lunch breaks, period configurations, and maximum limits. Cover the two-phase generation approach: lab course placement first, followed by theory course placement. Explain the fitness function, conflict detection mechanisms, and placement strategies. Include examples of constraint validation, slot allocation algorithms, and calendar occupancy tracking. Document the error handling for placement failures and the fallback mechanisms used when constraints cannot be satisfied.

wiki_catalogs.name: Constraint Satisfaction Algorithm
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the constraint satisfaction algorithm used in timetable generation. Detail the CSP solver implementation including hard constraints (room capacity, faculty availability, time conflicts) and soft constraints (preference matching, double period optimization). Explain the slot allocation mechanism, occupancy calendar tracking, and conflict detection algorithms. Document the constraint validation pipeline, including time slot generation, room availability checking, and faculty scheduling conflicts. Cover the priority-based course placement strategy, lab-first placement algorithm, and theory course distribution. Include implementation details of the fits() function, commit() operation, and the overall backtracking approach. Provide examples of constraint violations and their resolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Natural Language Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the natural language processing component of the AI optimization system. Document the Gemini API integration for processing academic queries and converting textual descriptions into actionable scheduling insights. Explain the prompt engineering strategies used for timetable analysis, constraint creation, and compliance validation. Detail the query processing pipeline including input parsing, context extraction, and response formatting. Cover the natural language understanding capabilities for academic scheduling domains, including NEP 2020 guidelines interpretation and constraint translation. Include examples of processed queries, response formats, and integration patterns with the constraint creation system. Document error handling, fallback mechanisms, and performance optimization techniques for natural language processing tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core Pydantic models that form the foundation of ShedMaster's data architecture. Detail the MongoBaseModel base class and its configuration for MongoDB integration, including ObjectId handling and JSON serialization. Document the User model hierarchy (UserBase, UserCreate, UserUpdate, UserInDB, User) with field definitions, validation rules, and security considerations. Explain the Constraint and Rule models that define scheduling restrictions and compliance requirements. Cover model inheritance patterns, shared validation logic, and common field configurations. Include examples of model instantiation, validation workflows, and database serialization. Address security aspects like password hashing, role-based access fields, and data sanitization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for ShedMaster. Include prerequisites covering Python 3.8+, Node.js 16+, MongoDB setup, and Google Gemini API key requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for both development and production environments. Document environment variable configuration including MONGODB_URI, GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY, and CORS settings. Explain the startup process using PowerShell scripts and Docker Compose. Include first-time setup procedures, initial admin user creation, and basic system verification. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common installation issues and environment configuration problems. Include verification steps to ensure all components are running correctly.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for component relationships within ShedMaster's architecture. Document how React components in the frontend communicate with FastAPI endpoints through HTTP requests and responses. Explain the relationship between TimetableContext and backend services for state synchronization. Detail the service layer architecture showing how generator.py integrates with constraint management, AI optimization, and template services. Document the dependency injection patterns and how components interact through well-defined interfaces. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user workflows from form submission to timetable generation. Explain the observer pattern implementation through React Query for real-time state updates. Address component lifecycle management and inter-component communication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Endpoints Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all ShedMaster backend endpoints. Document each endpoint group including authentication, user management, academic structure, faculty management, timetable generation, and AI services. For each endpoint, provide HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include detailed parameter descriptions, validation rules, and error response formats. Document the RESTful API design patterns used throughout the application. Cover pagination, filtering, sorting capabilities, and bulk operations where applicable. Include practical examples of API usage with curl commands and response samples. Address rate limiting, CORS configuration, and security considerations. Provide guidance on API versioning and backward compatibility maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features and Benefits
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content documenting the key features and benefits of the ShedMaster Academic Timetable System. Cover constraint-based timetable generation that automatically resolves scheduling conflicts. Explain the Google Gemini AI optimization that suggests improvements and detects potential issues. Detail the multi-format export capabilities including Excel, PDF, and JSON formats. Document the NEP 2020 compliance validation system that ensures educational guidelines adherence. Explain the faculty workload balancing algorithms that prevent over-scheduling. Cover the template-based generation system for consistent scheduling patterns. Detail the real-time collaboration features and draft saving capabilities. Include the academic program management system for organizing courses and student groups. Provide concrete examples of how each feature improves efficiency and reduces administrative burden. Address the measurable benefits such as reduced scheduling time, improved compliance rates, and enhanced satisfaction metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Migration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for ShedMaster's data migration and transformation processes. Document schema evolution patterns, version management strategies, and backward compatibility considerations. Explain migration scripts including fix_data_visibility.py for data consistency, reset.py for development environments, and cleanup_templates.py for maintenance operations. Detail data transformation procedures, bulk update operations, and atomic transaction patterns. Document backup and recovery procedures, data archival strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Include migration testing approaches, rollback procedures, and validation strategies. Address data integrity checks, consistency verification, and error handling during migrations. Provide step-by-step migration guides for common scenarios and troubleshooting procedures for migration failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Timetable Generation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the timetable generation engine including constraint satisfaction problem solving, genetic algorithm implementation, and template-based generation. Document the CSP solver algorithms used for conflict resolution and optimal assignment. Explain the advanced generation techniques including NEP 2020 compliance validation and performance optimization. Detail the template system for reusable generation patterns and batch processing capabilities. Cover the constraint validation pipeline, fitness evaluation functions, and solution quality metrics. Include implementation details of the genetic algorithm components, mutation and crossover operators, and convergence criteria. Provide examples of generation workflows and troubleshooting common constraint conflicts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the React component architecture. Document the main layout components including MainLayout, Navbar, Sidebar, and SimpleLayout with their props, styling, and usage patterns. Detail page components like Dashboard, Login, Signup, CreateTimetable, and Timetables with their specific functionality and data flow. wiki_catalogs.name: Authorization and Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authorization and permissions documentation for ShedMaster's role-based access control system. Document user roles including administrator, faculty member, and system user with their respective permissions and access levels. Explain the permission matrix for different operations such as timetable creation, modification, deletion, and viewing. Detail the implementation of protected routes using React components and backend API decorators. Cover dynamic permission checking for CRUD operations on academic data including programs, courses, faculty, and timetables. Document the AuthGuard component implementation for protecting frontend routes and the backend permission decorators for API endpoints. Include examples of permission-based UI rendering and conditional feature access. Address edge cases such as data visibility restrictions, collaborative editing permissions, and audit trail requirements. Provide guidelines for extending the permission system and adding new roles or permissions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed production deployment documentation for ShedMaster. Document Docker containerization strategy with multi-stage builds for optimal performance and security. Explain Docker Compose configuration for orchestrating backend, frontend, and MongoDB services. Detail environment configuration management including secrets handling, SSL certificate setup, and reverse proxy configuration. Cover cloud platform deployment strategies for AWS, Azure, and GCP including container orchestration with Kubernetes. Document CI/CD pipeline setup for automated deployment processes. Include load balancing configuration, health checks, and auto-scaling policies. Address security considerations including network policies, firewall configuration, and vulnerability scanning. Provide deployment validation procedures and rollback strategies for production releases.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management Endpoints

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Generation Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the advanced timetable generation algorithms including genetic algorithms and evolutionary computation. Document the GAEngine implementation with chromosome representation, fitness evaluation, crossover and mutation operators. Explain the NEP GA Engine specifically designed for NEP 2020 compliance with specialized fitness functions and constraints. Cover the advanced_generator.py implementation including hybrid approaches combining constraint satisfaction with evolutionary algorithms. Detail the optimization strategies, population management, selection mechanisms, and convergence criteria. Include examples of algorithm configuration, parameter tuning, and performance benchmarking. Document the integration between different algorithmic approaches and their respective use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Genetic Algorithm Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the genetic algorithm implementation used in advanced timetable optimization. Explain the chromosome representation encoding courses, rooms, faculty, and time slots. Document the fitness function calculation including penalty scoring for constraint violations, preference satisfaction, and NEP 2020 compliance metrics. Detail the selection mechanisms, crossover operators for timetable combinations, and mutation strategies for local optimization. Cover the population initialization, elitism preservation, and convergence criteria. Include implementation specifics of the NEP 2020 compliance scoring, double period optimization, and lunch break maximization. Provide examples of genetic operations and performance tuning parameters.

wiki_catalogs.name: Constraint Creation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the AI-driven constraint creation system that automatically generates scheduling rules from academic requirements. Document the constraint suggestion algorithms that analyze program specifications, faculty availability, and institutional guidelines to propose optimal scheduling constraints. Explain the integration with Gemini AI for intelligent constraint generation, including parameter extraction, priority assignment, and rationale generation. Detail the constraint validation mechanisms, conflict detection, and optimization strategies. Cover the constraint types supported including faculty workload limits, room capacity requirements, and NEP 2020 compliance rules. Include examples of constraint creation workflows, parameter tuning, and integration with the timetable generation engine. Document the constraint evaluation system and impact assessment methodologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack integration documentation for ShedMaster's full-stack architecture. Document the Python ecosystem integration including FastAPI, Pydantic, Motor, and ortools for constraint solving. Explain React 19 TypeScript integration with Material-UI, React Query for state management, and Vite for build optimization. Detail the MongoDB integration patterns including async operations, connection pooling, and schema validation. Document the AI service integration with Google Gemini API including authentication, rate limiting, and error handling. Explain CORS configuration, JWT token management, and security middleware integration. Address build tooling, dependency management, and development workflow integration. Include performance monitoring integration and logging framework coordination.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Logic Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the ShedMaster business logic layer focusing on timetable generation and management services. Document the constraint-based generation engine including algorithm implementation, conflict resolution strategies, and optimization techniques. Explain the advanced generation system with genetic algorithms and evolutionary computation. Detail the template-based generation service and its role in standardizing timetable creation. Cover the export functionality supporting multiple formats (Excel, PDF, JSON) and customization options. Document the NEP 2020 compliance engine and validation rules. Include performance optimization strategies, memory management, and scalability considerations. Provide examples of service integration patterns and dependency injection usage. Address error handling, logging, and debugging approaches for complex business operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for the ShedMaster system. Detail the backend technology choices including FastAPI for high-performance Python web framework, MongoDB for flexible document storage, and ortools for constraint satisfaction optimization. Document the frontend stack with React 19, Material-UI for component design, and Vite for build tooling. Explain the AI integration with Google Gemini API for natural language processing and optimization suggestions. Detail the development tools including TypeScript for type safety, React Query for state management, and Docker for containerization. Document the database technology with Motor for async MongoDB operations and Pydantic for data validation. Include the build and deployment technologies such as npm scripts, PowerShell startup scripts, and GitHub Actions. Explain the rationale behind each technology choice and how they complement each other. Address version compatibility and upgrade paths. Provide setup requirements and environment configuration details.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management and Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the data management and export functionality covering timetable storage, retrieval, and multi-format export capabilities. Explain the data persistence layer, indexing strategies, and query optimization for timetable data. Detail the export system supporting Excel, PDF, and JSON formats with customizable templates. Cover the import functionality for academic data including CSV parsing and validation. Document the data validation pipeline, integrity checks, and error handling mechanisms. Include the API endpoints for timetable CRUD operations, search functionality, and bulk operations. Explain the frontend integration patterns for data display, filtering, and user interaction. Provide examples of data export workflows, import validation scenarios, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface Components

Detail the multi-step CreateTimetable form components including AcademicStructureTab, CourseInformationTabNew, FacultyDataTab, InfrastructureDataTab, RoomsTab, StudentGroupsTab, TimeConstraintsTab, TimeRulesTab, and TimetableDisplay. wiki_catalogs.name: Data Validation and Serialization

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ShedMaster: AI-Powered Academic Timetable Generation System

## Project Introduction

**ShedMaster** is a comprehensive academic timetable generation system designed to streamline the complex process of creating efficient and NEP 2020 compliant timetables for educational institutions. The platform combines constraint-based algorithms with AI-powered optimization to deliver intelligent scheduling solutions that balance faculty workloads, room utilization, and student learning requirements.

### Purpose Statement
The system addresses the challenges faced by educational institutions in manual timetable creation, including time-consuming processes, human errors, and difficulty in maintaining compliance with NEP 2020 guidelines. ShedMaster automates this process while ensuring academic standards and institutional requirements are met.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Constraint-Based Generation**: Implement sophisticated algorithms that respect institutional constraints and academic requirements
- **AI Optimization**: Leverage Google Gemini AI for intelligent timetable optimization and conflict resolution
- **NEP 2020 Compliance**: Ensure all generated timetables meet the guidelines of India's National Education Policy 2020
- **User-Friendly Interface**: Provide an intuitive web-based interface for easy timetable management
- **Multi-Format Export**: Support various export formats for seamless integration with existing systems

### Target Audience
- Educational institutions (colleges, universities, schools)
- Academic administrators and planners
- Faculty members requiring schedule management tools
- Students needing transparent timetable access

## Technical Architecture

The ShedMaster system follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation of concerns between frontend, backend, and database layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend React Application] --> B[FastAPI Backend Server]
    B --> C[MongoDB Database]
    B --> D[Google Gemini AI API]
    A --> E[RESTful API Endpoints]
    F[React Query] --> E
    G[Zustand State Management] --> A
    H[Material-UI Components] --> A
    
    subgraph "Frontend Layer"
        A
        F
        G
        H
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Layer"
        B
        C
        D
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Layer"
        C
    end
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture**
- **React 19** with TypeScript for type-safe development
- **Vite** for fast development and build processes
- **Material-UI** for responsive and accessible UI components
- **React Router** for client-side navigation
- **React Query** for server state management and caching
- **Zustand** for lightweight state management

**Backend Architecture**
- **FastAPI** for high-performance Python web framework
- **MongoDB** for flexible document-based data storage
- **Pydantic** for data validation and serialization
- **Uvicorn** for ASGI server deployment

**AI Integration**
- **Google Gemini API** for intelligent optimization and suggestions
- **Genetic Algorithm Engine** for advanced timetable optimization
- **Constraint Satisfaction Algorithms** for rule-based generation

### Design Patterns

The system implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracts database operations through service layers
- **Factory Pattern**: Creates different types of timetable generators based on requirements
- **Observer Pattern**: Manages real-time updates through React Query
- **Strategy Pattern**: Allows switching between different generation algorithms

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Frontend
    participant Backend
    participant Database
    participant AI_API
    
    User->>Frontend: Create Timetable Request
    Frontend->>Backend: API Call (POST /timetable/generate)
    Backend->>Database: Load Academic Data
    Backend->>AI_API: Request Optimization
    AI_API-->>Backend: AI Generated Suggestions
    Backend->>Database: Store Timetable
    Database-->>Backend: Confirmation
    Backend-->>Frontend: Timetable Response
    Frontend-->>User: Display Timetable
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Entry Point**
- `backend/app/main.py` - Central application configuration with FastAPI setup, CORS middleware, and route registration

**Frontend Entry Point**
- `frontend/src/App.tsx` - React application root with routing, theming, and authentication providers

### Core Modules

**API Layer**
- `backend/app/api/api_v1/api.py` - Central router configuration organizing all API endpoints by domain
- `backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/` - Modular endpoint implementations for different entities (users, timetable, programs, etc.)

**Business Logic**
- `backend/app/services/timetable/generator.py` - Core constraint-based timetable generation engine
- `backend/app/services/ai/gemini.py` - AI-powered optimization and analysis services
- `backend/app/services/timetable/template_service.py` - Template-based generation system

**Data Models**
- `backend/app/models/` - Pydantic models defining data structures for validation and serialization
- `backend/app/db/mongodb.py` - MongoDB connection and collection management

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `backend/app/core/config.py` - Centralized settings management with environment variable support
- `.env` file for sensitive configuration data

**Frontend Configuration**
- `frontend/package.json` - Dependency management and build scripts
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` - Development server and build configuration

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- `backend/requirements.txt` - Core dependencies including FastAPI, MongoDB drivers, and AI libraries
- Key packages: FastAPI, Motor (async MongoDB), PyJWT, bcrypt, ortools (for optimization), google-generativeai

**Frontend Dependencies**
- `frontend/package.json` - Modern frontend stack with React 19, Material-UI, and supporting libraries
- Key packages: React 19, React Router, Material-UI, React Query, Axios

### Integration Points

**Database Integration**
- MongoDB Atlas for cloud database hosting
- Async operations for improved performance
- Connection pooling and error handling

**AI Integration**
- Google Gemini API for intelligent optimization
- Structured prompt engineering for timetable analysis
- Response parsing and validation

**External Services**
- RESTful API communication between frontend and backend
- Authentication and authorization middleware
- CORS configuration for cross-origin requests

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[TimetableContext] --> B[CreateTimetable Component]
    B --> C[Tab Components]
    C --> D[Form Validation]
    D --> E[API Service]
    E --> F[Backend API]
    F --> G[Database Operations]
    
    subgraph "State Management Flow"
        A --> D
        D --> E
    end
    
    subgraph "Data Flow"
        G --> F
        F --> E
        E --> B
    end
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Constraint-Based Generation**
- Intelligent algorithm that respects institutional constraints and academic requirements
- Handles complex scheduling scenarios including lab sessions, faculty availability, and room capacity
- Supports multiple generation strategies (basic, advanced, NEP-compliant)

**AI-Powered Optimization**
- Google Gemini integration for intelligent timetable analysis and suggestions
- Automated conflict detection and resolution
- Performance scoring and improvement recommendations

**Multi-Format Export**
- Excel spreadsheet export for easy sharing and modification
- PDF generation for formal documentation
- JSON format for system integration

**Real-Time Collaboration**
- Draft saving with automatic persistence
- Progress tracking across multiple setup stages
- Version control and audit trails

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced Generation Engine**
- `backend/app/services/timetable/generator.py` - Sophisticated constraint satisfaction algorithm
- Handles theory and lab courses with different time requirements
- Implements genetic algorithm optimization for NEP 2020 compliance

**Intelligent AI Services**
- `backend/app/services/ai/gemini.py` - Comprehensive AI assistance for timetable optimization
- Natural language processing for user queries
- Compliance validation against NEP 2020 guidelines

**Modern Frontend Architecture**
- `frontend/src/components/pages/CreateTimetable.tsx` - Multi-step guided timetable creation
- Real-time validation and error handling
- Responsive design with Material-UI components

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> AcademicSetup
    AcademicSetup --> CourseManagement
    CourseManagement --> FacultyManagement
    FacultyManagement --> StudentGroups
    StudentGroups --> RoomManagement
    RoomManagement --> TimeConstraints
    TimeConstraints --> Generation
    Generation --> Review
    Review --> Export
    Export --> [*]
    
    Generation --> AIAssistance
    AIAssistance --> Generation
    Review --> AIAssistance
```

**Template-Based Generation**
- Pre-configured templates for different academic programs
- Rule-based customization for specific institutional requirements
- Smart slot allocation considering practical and theoretical needs

**Validation and Quality Assurance**
- Automatic constraint checking during generation
- NEP 2020 compliance verification
- Performance metrics and optimization suggestions

The ShedMaster system represents a comprehensive solution for academic timetable management, combining traditional constraint satisfaction methods with modern AI capabilities to deliver efficient, compliant, and user-friendly scheduling solutions for educational institutions.

---

**Sources:**
- [main.py](backend/app/main.py)
- [config.py](backend/app/core/config.py)
- [api.py](backend/app/api/api_v1/api.py)
- [App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [requirements.txt](backend/requirements.txt)
- [timetable.py](backend/app/models/timetable.py)
- [generator.py](backend/app/services/timetable/generator.py)
- [gemini.py](backend/app/services/ai/gemini.py)
- [CreateTimetable.tsx](frontend/src/components/pages/CreateTimetable.tsx)
- [TimetableContext.tsx](frontend/src/contexts/TimetableContext.tsx)
- [timetable.py](backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/timetable.py)
- [timetableService.ts](frontend/src/services/timetableService.ts)

</blog>
